  • Exports

Imports

strategies/base, declarative/risk_management

Types

StrategyBuilder = ref object
Builder for configuring strategies with risk management

Procs

proc build(sb: StrategyBuilder): Strategy {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Build and return the configured strategy

Applies risk management configuration to strategy and returns it.

Returns: Configured strategy instance

proc newStrategyBuilder(strategy: Strategy): StrategyBuilder {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Create a new strategy builder

Args: strategy: Strategy instance to configure

Returns: New StrategyBuilder instance

proc withATRStop(sb: StrategyBuilder; multiplier: float;
                 indicatorId: string = "atr_14"): StrategyBuilder {.
    ...raises: [RiskManagementError], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Add ATR-based stop-loss (volatility-adjusted)

Stop distance adapts to market volatility using ATR indicator. Requires strategy to implement getIndicatorValue() method.

Args: multiplier: ATR multiplier for stop distance indicatorId: ID of ATR indicator (default "atr_14")

Returns: Builder instance for chaining

Example: # Stop at 2x ATR below entry strategy.withATRStop(multiplier = 2.0)

proc withFixedStopLoss(sb: StrategyBuilder; percentage: float): StrategyBuilder {.
    ...raises: [RiskManagementError], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Add fixed percentage stop-loss

Exits position when loss reaches specified percentage below entry price.

Args: percentage: Loss percentage to trigger stop (e.g., 5.0 = 5%)

Returns: Builder instance for chaining

Example: strategy.withFixedStopLoss(5.0) # Exit at 5% loss

proc withFixedStopLoss(strategy: Strategy; percentage: float): Strategy {.
    ...raises: [RiskManagementError], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Convenience: Add fixed percentage stop-loss to strategy

Args: strategy: Strategy instance percentage: Stop-loss percentage

Returns: Same strategy instance (for chaining)

Example: let strategy = newRSIStrategy(14, 30.0, 70.0) .withFixedStopLoss(5.0) .withFixedTakeProfit(10.0)

proc withFixedTakeProfit(sb: StrategyBuilder; percentage: float): StrategyBuilder {.
    ...raises: [RiskManagementError], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Add fixed percentage take-profit

Exits entire position when profit reaches specified percentage.

Args: percentage: Profit percentage to trigger exit (e.g., 10.0 = 10%)

Returns: Builder instance for chaining

Example: strategy.withFixedTakeProfit(10.0) # Exit at 10% profit

proc withFixedTakeProfit(strategy: Strategy; percentage: float): Strategy {.
    ...raises: [RiskManagementError], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Convenience: Add fixed percentage take-profit to strategy

Args: strategy: Strategy instance percentage: Take-profit percentage

Returns: Same strategy instance (for chaining)

Example: let strategy = newRSIStrategy(14, 30.0, 70.0) .withFixedStopLoss(5.0) .withFixedTakeProfit(10.0)

proc withMultiLevelProfit(sb: StrategyBuilder; levels: seq[TakeProfitLevel]): StrategyBuilder {.
    ...raises: [RiskManagementError], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Add multi-level take-profit (partial exits)

Allows scaling out of positions at multiple profit levels. Each level specifies a profit target and what % of position to exit.

Args: levels: Sequence of take-profit levels

Returns: Builder instance for chaining

Example: # Exit 50% at 5% profit, remaining 50% at 10% profit strategy.withMultiLevelProfit(@ TakeProfitLevel(percentage: 5.0, exitPercent: 50.0), TakeProfitLevel(percentage: 10.0, exitPercent: 50.0) )

proc withPriceStopLoss(sb: StrategyBuilder; price: float): StrategyBuilder {.
    ...raises: [RiskManagementError], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Add fixed price stop-loss

Exits position when price falls to or below specified level.

Args: price: Absolute price level for stop

Returns: Builder instance for chaining

Example: strategy.withPriceStopLoss(95.0) # Exit at $95

proc withPriceTakeProfit(sb: StrategyBuilder; price: float): StrategyBuilder {.
    ...raises: [RiskManagementError], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Add fixed price take-profit

Exits position when price reaches specified level.

Args: price: Absolute price level for take-profit

Returns: Builder instance for chaining

Example: strategy.withPriceTakeProfit(110.0) # Exit at $110

proc withRiskManagement(strategy: Strategy; stopLoss: StopLossRule = nil;
                        takeProfit: TakeProfitRule = nil): Strategy {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Convenience function to add risk management directly to a strategy

This provides a simpler API when you don't need the builder pattern.

Args: strategy: Strategy instance stopLoss: Stop-loss rule (nil = no stop-loss) takeProfit: Take-profit rule (nil = no take-profit)

Returns: Same strategy instance (for chaining)

Example: let strategy = newRSIStrategy(14, 30.0, 70.0) .withRiskManagement( stopLoss = newFixedPercentageStopLoss(5.0), takeProfit = newFixedPercentageTakeProfit(10.0) )

proc withRiskReward(sb: StrategyBuilder; ratio: float): StrategyBuilder {.
    ...raises: [ValueError, RiskManagementError], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Add risk/reward ratio take-profit

Profit target is calculated as: entry + (stop_distance * ratio) Requires stop-loss to be configured first.

Args: ratio: Risk/reward ratio (e.g., 2.0 = take profit at 2x stop distance)

Returns: Builder instance for chaining

Raises: ValueError if no stop-loss is configured

Example: # 5% stop with 2:1 risk/reward = 10% take-profit strategy.withFixedStopLoss(5.0).withRiskReward(2.0)

proc withStopLoss(sb: StrategyBuilder; rule: StopLossRule): StrategyBuilder {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Add a custom stop-loss rule

Args: rule: Stop-loss rule instance

Returns: Builder instance for chaining

proc withTakeProfit(sb: StrategyBuilder; rule: TakeProfitRule): StrategyBuilder {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Add a custom take-profit rule

Args: rule: Take-profit rule instance

Returns: Builder instance for chaining

proc withTrailingStop(sb: StrategyBuilder; trailPct: float;
                      activationPct: float = 0.0): StrategyBuilder {.
    ...raises: [RiskManagementError], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Add trailing stop-loss

Stop follows price up at specified distance, locking in profits. Optionally waits for minimum profit before activating.

Args: trailPct: Distance to trail behind high (%) activationPct: Minimum profit before trailing starts (default 0)

Returns: Builder instance for chaining

Example: # Trail 3% behind high, activate immediately strategy.withTrailingStop(3.0)

Trail 3% behind high, activate after 5% profit

strategy.withTrailingStop(trailPct = 3.0, activationPct = 5.0)

proc withTrailingStop(strategy: Strategy; trailPct: float;
                      activationPct: float = 0.0): Strategy {.
    ...raises: [RiskManagementError], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Convenience: Add trailing stop to strategy

Args: strategy: Strategy instance trailPct: Trail percentage activationPct: Activation profit percentage

Returns: Same strategy instance (for chaining)

Exports

checkStopLoss, newFixedPriceStopLoss, FixedPercentageStopLoss, checkStopLoss, newFixedPercentageTakeProfit, calculateStopPrice, TakeProfitKind, ATRBasedStopLoss, newNoTakeProfit, markLevelHit, newMultiLevelTakeProfit, newRiskRewardTakeProfit, newNoStopLoss, checkStopLoss, newATRBasedStopLoss, checkTakeProfit, checkTakeProfit, TrailingStopLoss, checkStopLoss, checkStopLoss, TakeProfitLevel, PositionState, FixedPercentageTakeProfit, newFixedPercentageStopLoss, checkTakeProfit, newPositionState, StopLossKind, calculateTakeProfitPrice, checkTakeProfit, checkTakeProfit, StopLossRule, FixedPriceTakeProfit, RiskRewardTakeProfit, TakeProfitRule, RiskManagementError, newTrailingStopLoss, FixedPriceStopLoss, MultiLevelTakeProfit, newFixedPriceTakeProfit, updateState
